$comment: Neutral shipped <sys/mman.h> (POSIX) — c14 (linux) + c15c (macOS). The memory-mapped-I/O surface SQLite's os_unix.c uses for its mmap'd page cache: mmap/munmap + Linux mremap (resize, under SQLITE_MMAP_READWRITE). PROT_READ/PROT_WRITE (page protections) + MAP_SHARED all AGREE between Linux and macOS (1/2/1 — verified against xnu/bsd/sys/mman.h), so they stay flat. mremap + MREMAP_MAYMOVE are LINUX-ONLY: macOS has no mremap(). Neither is injected on a macho target — MREMAP_MAYMOVE via an elf-only constant `variants` entry, the mremap SYMBOL via a per-symbol `availableObjectFormats` of elf-only (DSS imports EVERY declared shipped extern, so a macho-declared mremap would plant an undefined import; libSystem has no mremap). A macho reference to either fails loud (truthful). Both are present on elf for linux SQLite's use. (D-SHIPPED-SYMBOL-PER-TARGET-AVAILABILITY) MAP_FAILED is the `((void*)-1)` sentinel (a pointer → the MACROS surface). off_t is i64 (LP64, both). Gated availableObjectFormats:[elf,macho]: elf=the WSL run target, macho=the macos-latest CI leg (Apple Silicon).

symbols.name: mmap
symbols.signature: fn(ptr<void>, u64, i32, i32, i32, i64) -> ptr<void>
